summary: Elder Neil L. Andersen teaches that eternal marriage, made possible through Christ’s Resurrection and restored sealing power, is a sacred covenant journey of becoming more like the Savior through faith, patience, sacrifice, and enduring love.
haiku:
Hands clasped at the veil
Covenants weather all storms
Love walks toward the dawn
key_points: The sealing power restored through Jesus Christ binds faithful spouses and families together eternally.
Eternal marriage is not merely a ceremony but a lifelong and eternal journey of spiritual refinement and Christlike becoming.
Couples need the Holy Ghost, patience, repentance, forgiveness, and hope in Christ to navigate trials and remain faithful to covenants.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Luke 24:5–6 — “He is not here, but is risen”
Matthew 16:19 — The power to bind on earth and in heaven
Isaiah 40:28–31 — The Lord gives power to the faint and strength to those who wait upon Him
The sealing power and temple covenants
The doctrine of eternal marriage and eternal families
The law of chastity and preparation for temple covenants
The gift of the Holy Ghost as protection against deception
Christlike attributes within marriage: sacrifice, charity, patience, peacemaking, repentance, and forgiveness
“The Family: A Proclamation to the World” — Marriage between a man and a woman is ordained of God
President Gordon B. Hinckley’s teaching on the worth of the restored sealing power
questions_to_ponder: How does the Savior’s Resurrection make eternal marriage and eternal families possible?
In what ways can marriage become a “crucible of spiritual development”?
What Christlike attributes is the Lord helping me develop through my closest relationships?
How can couples keep their temple covenants central during times of disappointment, illness, or sorrow?
Why is the influence of the Holy Ghost essential in marriage and family life?
How can hope in Jesus Christ strengthen those who are not yet sealed or whose family circumstances are difficult?
